title: For a struggling Red Sox team, Friday’s opener at Fenway Park provided a fresh start
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Boston Red Sox won 5-2 against the San Diego Padres in their home opener at Fenway Park.
Caleb Durbin and Willson Contreras contributed key hits, helping the Red Sox to a much-needed victory.
Sonny Gray pitched six strong innings, boosting the Red Sox's confidence after their 2-5 season start.

title: North Andover police working to get body cameras after Kelsey Fitzsimmons shooting
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover plans to implement a body camera program following the shooting incident involving officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons.
Town officials seek grant funding to help reduce taxpayer costs for introducing the police body-worn camera program.
Judge highlighted the absence of body cameras during Fitzsimmons's trial, suggesting it influenced the reasonable doubt in the verdict.

title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park as a Padres player, reflecting on changes since his Red Sox departure.
Bogaerts, who signed an 11-year, $280 million deal with San Diego, mentioned the pressure of high fan expectations.
Bogaerts sees Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony, 21, as the team's new face after signing an eight-year contract.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Freedom Trail in Boston spans 2.5 miles, connects 16 historical sites, and attracts over 4 million visitors annually.
Six Flags New England introduces the Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, opening April 17 to the public.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast offers prix fixe menus priced at $32, $42, and $52 from April 9-18.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton's noise complaint against neighbors reached the Rhode Island Supreme Court over alleged excessive noise and harassment.
Carlton and husband John McCauley won an injunction in 2024 limiting noise levels, which neighbors Avagyan and Beausoleil appealed.
Avagyan and Beausoleil argue the injunction is vague and challenge a zoning board decision on excessive property use.

title: Delta is adding winter service between Boston and Rome in 2027
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Delta Air Lines will add winter flights between Boston and Rome starting January 2027.
The service will run four times a week from January before resuming daily in March.
Tickets for the new service go on sale April 4, 2026.

title: He’s running his second Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Joe Greeley, 30, from Charlestown, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women's Hospital's Stepping Strong.
Greeley previously ran the Boston Marathon in 2024 and was inspired to participate again after experiencing FOMO in 2025.
He appreciates the support from friends and the Stepping Strong team, encouraging donations to the cause he races for.

title: ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Everything that happened in Episode 1
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premiered with drama including throuple issues, wedding pressure, and beach confrontations.
The episode features cast members Liz McGraw and Alicia Carmody dealing with family and relationship complexities.
A picnic organized by Rosie Woods leads to tensions with Jo-Ellen Tiberi, causing a heated argument.

title: 8 gorgeous places in New England to see daffodils this spring
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Newport hosts Daffodil Days featuring over 1.5 million daffodils, trolley tours, and various events in April.
Naumkeag in Stockbridge offers an 8-acre garden festival with daffodils, tulips, and seasonal activities from April 18 to May 10.
The Meriden Daffodil Festival in Connecticut on May 2-3 features 600,000 daffodils, live music, and children's activities.

title: Watch the Red Sox Opening Day ceremony honoring 1986 team
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox honor the 1986 team on Opening Day at Fenway Park, celebrating their American League pennant win.
Nearly 25 players from the 1986 Red Sox team return for the 40th anniversary celebration on Friday.
The ceremony includes a performance of 'The Star-Spangled Banner' by Broadway actor Nik Walker.
